data.output:
ID:       ddx-6d132f62
Title:    Story 16.0: Backend run-detail resolver + tool-call persistence + bundle-file confinement
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:16
Parent:   ddx-a8a18cb7
Desc:     Persist normalized ToolCallEntry at drain time onto AgentSession/Run detail backing (extend existing drain in service_run.go / agent_runner_service.go). Extend Run GraphQL resolver with: prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[] (path, size, mimeType), bundleFile(path) -> {content, truncated, sizeBytes}. Bundle-root confinement: canonical-path check rejecting any path outside <projectRoot>/.ddx/executions/<runId>/. Whitelist enforcement for inline content (allowed extensions: *.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json; <64KB). Resolver unit tests cover: path traversal (.., symlink, absolute), size cap (>64KB returns truncated=true with sizeBytes), whitelist enforcement, tool-call persistence-at-drain (not log parsing). See /tmp/story-16-final.md.
Accept:   Run resolver exposes prompt/response/stderr/toolCalls(first,after)/bundleFiles[]/bundleFile(path). ToolCallEntry persisted at drain time (not parsed from logs at resolver time). bundleFile rejects path traversal, symlink escape, absolute paths outside bundle root with 404. Files >64KB or non-whitelisted extensions return truncated=true,sizeBytes=N (no content). Unit tests cover all of the above. Existing drain code in service_run.go / agent_runner_service.go updated to write normalized ToolCallEntry to detail backing.
Created:  2026-05-02 12:15:45
Updated:  2026-05-02 12:15:45
---
ID:       ddx-f04faa86
Title:    Story 16.1: Frontend rebuild runs/[runId]/+page.svelte as tabbed run-detail page
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:16
Parent:   ddx-a8a18cb7
Deps:     ddx-6d132f62
Desc:     Promote shared subcomponents from cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/executions/[executionId]/+page.svelte into lib/runDetail/ (tab shell, prompt panel, response panel, tools panel, session panel). Rebuild runs/[runId]/+page.svelte as tabbed: Overview / Prompt / Response / Tools / Session (Evidence tab handled in 16.2). URL-driven tab state via ?tab=<name>; deep-link friendly. Overview: status (running/merged/preserved/failed) with last-update timestamp, run/try/work layer chip, base/result rev, link to originating worker page for running runs (re-use existing workerProgress; no new subscription infra). Tools tab paginated 50/page with Load more. Tools tab lazy-loads on activation. Session tab shows tokens (prompt/completion/total/cached), billingMode, base/result revs, stderr or stderrPath link. Page e2e: tab switching + URL deep-link round-trip. Depends on 16.0 (resolver) and Story 8.2 (row-expansion port).
Accept:   /nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/[runId] renders tabbed: Overview/Prompt/Response/Tools/Session. Prompt tab shows full prompt body for both layer=run and layer=try. Response tab shows full response (run-layer) or result.json body (try-layer) with verdict badge. Tools tab paginates 50/page from persisted ToolCallEntry; Load more works. Session tab shows tokens/billingMode/revs/stderr. Tab state in ?tab= URL query; refresh restores. Running runs show status + 'View live progress' link to worker page. Tools tab lazy-loads. Shared subcomponents extracted to lib/runDetail/ and reused by /executions/[id] page. Page e2e covers tab switching + deep-link round-trip.
Created:  2026-05-02 12:16:00
Updated:  2026-05-02 12:16:25

ID:       ddx-4cd64068
Title:    Story 16.2: Evidence tab — bundle-file list + download + whitelist inline view
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:16
Parent:   ddx-a8a18cb7
Deps:     ddx-6d132f62, ddx-f04faa86
Desc:     Add Evidence tab to runs/[runId]/+page.svelte. Lists every file in run's bundle with path, size, mimeType. Whitelisted small text files (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, <64KB) render inline as raw text. Everything else: download link only (no Markdown rendering, no syntax highlighting, no hex preview). Evidence tab lazy-loads on activation. Large files (>64KB) show size + download button only. Evidence-specific e2e: whitelist enforcement (small whitelisted file inlines, large file download-only, non-whitelisted extension download-only), large-file download path, missing-path 404. Depends on 16.0 (bundleFile resolver) and 16.1 (page shell).
Accept:   Evidence tab lists every file in run's bundle with size + mimeType. Whitelisted text files <64KB render inline as raw text; non-whitelisted or >64KB files show download link only. Evidence tab lazy-loads. e2e covers: whitelist enforcement, large-file download, missing-path 404. p95 dataset (30 evidence files) renders without crash.
Created:  2026-05-02 12:16:08
Updated:  2026-05-02 12:16:25

Title:    Story 16.3: Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit event
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2

Desc:     Spec updates: FEAT-008 — add Run-detail-page subsection; document trust boundary statement ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args. Operators must not store production secrets in repos consumed by DDx agents'); document evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap). FEAT-010 — extend Run resolver contract to include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain. FEAT-019 — review surface points to /runs/[runId] deep page. Cross-cutting e2e: full prompt visible, full response visible, tool trace visible, evidence list visible — exercising the entire stack from drain through resolver through page render. Audit event for 'raw transcript viewed' (project-membership trust boundary documented; this audit is the only enforcement surface in v1). Depends on 16.1, 16.2.
Accept:   FEAT-008 updated with run-detail subsection + trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist. FEAT-010 updated with extended Run resolver contract + drain-time persistence note. FEAT-019 updated with link to /runs/[runId] from review surface. Cross-cutting e2e exercises full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end. 'Raw transcript viewed' audit event emitted on access and covered by test. p95 dataset (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) tab regression e2e verifies all tabs render and become interactive within one render frame.

Updated:  2026-05-02 12:16:25

input.content:
Bead ddx-a8a18cb7 is an orchestration epic for Story 16. Its sole acceptance
criterion is "All child beads closed. Locked decisions from the planning round
honored." There is no implementation work owned by the epic itself — every
testable AC lives on the four child beads:

  - ddx-6d132f62 (Story 16.0: backend resolver + tool-call persistence + bundle confinement)
  - ddx-f04faa86 (Story 16.1: frontend tabbed run-detail page; deps 16.0)
  - ddx-4cd64068 (Story 16.2: Evidence tab; deps 16.0, 16.1)
  - ddx-70a432b5 (Story 16.3: specs + cross-cutting e2e + audit event; deps 16.1, 16.2)

Status check at execution time: all four children are still `open`. The epic
cannot legitimately close until those close, and the prior review correctly
returned REVIEW:BLOCK on a previous attempt that produced an empty result.json
without advancing any child.

The right thing for the queue to do is execute the children (in dependency
order: 16.0 -> 16.1 -> 16.2 -> 16.3), then re-queue this epic so the closure
gate flips green. No code change is appropriate here; doing implementation
work under the epic ID would land changes that should belong to a child bead's
attempt and confuse traceability.

What is done:
  - Decomposition is already recorded in the parent's notes (see bead notes).
  - All four children exist with focused AC and the correct dependency edges.

What is blocking:
  - Children ddx-6d132f62, ddx-f04faa86, ddx-4cd64068, ddx-70a432b5 are open.

What a follow-up attempt needs:
  - Run the children to completion in dependency order, then re-attempt this
    epic. At that point the AC ("all child beads closed") becomes verifiable
    by `ddx bead show` on each child returning status=closed, and the epic
    can close without any code change.

data.text: Epic bead — all four children (ddx-6d132f62, ddx-f04faa86, ddx-4cd64068, ddx-70a432b5) are still open, so the AC ("all child beads closed") is unsatisfiable in this attempt. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining the situation; the queue should execute the children in dependency order (16.0 → 16.1 → 16.2 → 16.3) before re-attempting this epic. No commit.
